## prompt-draft-narrative

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-narrative.md`
(SUB-PROMPT SCHEDULE item 2), adapted from
`single-prompt-bill/auto-bill-02/sub-prompts/prompt-5-draft-bill.md` and the
table-of-contents and back-matter conventions of
`cancer-automated/tree/main/papers/VVUQ-02/final-paper`.*

Write the draft-narrative (scaffold) LaTeX into
`law-physical-ai-trials/review/draft-narrative/`, building on the existing
template in `law-physical-ai-trials/review/template` (preserve its visual
appearance exactly: the `#8B2E3F` accent, Linux Libertine body, the title rule,
the abstract box, the ORCID cover mark, and the `L`/`C`/`Y` table column types).
The draft is a scaffold: it lays out the full manuscript structure and, in every
body section, provides bracketed `[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS: ...]` that name the
exact repository files and directories that the full-narrative and
final-narrative stages must process, rather than finished prose.

**Structure (one `sections/*.tex` file per section, each `\input` by
`main.tex`).** Front matter (title page on the template theme, abstract,
keywords, disclaimer); an Introduction that defines the lay terms a medical-AI
legislator needs (Physical AI, surgical humanoid, VVUQ, verification before
generation, Claude Code and Codex peer review, the ten gates, ACCEPT / ESCALATE
/ BLOCK) and states the three guiding questions; a clickable, page-filling
`\tableofcontents`; the **eight body sections** mapped to the SECTION GOALS, in
order:

1. `s1-compassion.tex` - Compassion and empathy (patients, families, children
   with rare disease, access; "what happens if we do nothing?").
2. `s2-fear-of-harm.tex` - Fear of preventable harm (missed diagnoses, medical
   error, compounding human error, delayed treatment).
3. `s3-moral-outrage.tex` - Moral outrage (unequal access, denials, injustice
   that "should not happen in America").
4. `s4-hope.tex` - Hope (earlier detection, reduced burnout, rural access,
   faster discovery; "we can save lives if we act").
5. `s5-responsibility.tex` - Responsibility and duty (directed at the
   legislator; "you have the power to prevent this").
6. `s6-protection.tex` - Protection of vulnerable people (children, elderly,
   disabled, veterans, rural; bias, consent).
7. `s7-trust.tex` - Trust and reassurance (physician endorsement, testing,
   transparency, pilot results, audit trails).
8. `s8-urgency.tex` - Urgency (patients waiting now; technology outpacing
   regulation).

Then back matter adapted from VVUQ-02 final-paper: References,
Acknowledgments, Ethical Disclosures, Rights and Permissions, Cite This Article,
and Data Availability, each added to the table of contents with
`\phantomsection`.

**Bracketed drafting instructions must name exact sources**, for example:
the Bill itself (`review/references/author_works.bib` entry
`Kawchak2026HR9510Bill`, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762); the prior-work lineage in
`review/references/author_works.bib`; the verification evidence in
`cancer-automated/papers/VVUQ-02/` (172 of 172 tests, ten-gate suite); the
adverse-event SOP genre in `review/template`; the colored Mermaid catalog in
`review/mermaid/`; and the advocacy-science references carried into
`review/references/narrative_refs.bib` (`ingber2025regulating`,
`moreland2015hearing`, `novak2020patient`, `putturaj2025honouring`,
`kok2013finding`). Each body section names which Mermaid figure slot and which
table it will carry in the full stage.

**Figures and tables.** Define the colored TikZ Mermaid primitive in the style
file (the `narrativefig` environment and the themed node styles in the strict
palette) and place at least one labeled figure float or table per section, with
captions, even if the draft renders a labeled placeholder. Tables use
`>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` columns summing to `\textwidth`.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty` style file, `references.bib`, and
`README.md`; one commit per `sections/*.tex` file. Also commit
`prompt-draft-narrative.md` (this prompt verbatim under a
`## prompt-draft-narrative` heading) and `output-draft-narrative.md` (the
narrative output). For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across all
files; for the last commit write the directory README and the
`draft-narrative-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request.

**Formatting (centralize in the `.sty`).** White background; RaggedRight body
with even interword spacing and no right-margin overflow; widow and club
penalties at 10000 so no single line is stranded and no one or two word last
line is left behind; correct "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only (no en, em,
double, or triple dashes); `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and `\hspace` used as a
senior author would to balance pages. The scaffold must compile in Overleaf with
pdfLaTeX. Commit and push in real time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask
questions, or go into plan mode.
